channel.title: Home on Eau Claire Buddhist Sangha
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Eau Claire Buddhist Sangha

item.title: Tips for Meditation Posture
item.description:
After struggling with lower back pain during sits for almost two years, I’ve finally found a setup that works. Thought I’d share in case it helps others.

What helped me

  1. A higher cushion — I switched from a standard zafu to a taller one (about 6 inches). This tilted my pelvis forward enough that my spine could stack naturally.

  2. A folded blanket under my knees — For those of us who sit cross-legged, a little support under the knees takes pressure off the hips.

item.title: Weekly Tuesday Sit & Dharma Talk
item.description:
Our core weekly gathering. We begin with 30 minutes of silent meditation, followed by a talk on “The Second Noble Truth: The Cause of Suffering”.

Schedule

  • 6:45 PM - Optional meditation instruction for beginners
  • 7:00 PM - Silent sitting meditation (30 minutes)
  • 7:30 PM - Dharma talk or discussion
  • 8:00 PM - Tea and open discussion

All levels of experience are welcome. Cushions and chairs are provided. No special clothing needed.

item.title: Daily Practice Struggles
item.description:
How do you all maintain a 20-minute daily sit when the kids are home? I’ve been struggling to find consistent time in the mornings before work.

I’ve tried:

  • Waking up 30 minutes earlier (hard when the kids wake up early too)
  • Sitting in the evening (I’m too tired)
  • Short 10-minute sits (helpful but not the same)

Would love to hear what works for other parents in the sangha.

item.title: New Library Books Available
item.description:
We have added five new titles to our community library, including Pema Chödrön’s latest work on living with compassion and uncertainty.

New titles include:

  • Living Beautifully with Uncertainty and Change by Pema Chödrön
  • The Art of Gathering by Priya Krishna
  • Stillness Speaks by Eckhart Tolle

Feel free to browse and borrow after our Tuesday meetings. Our library is a free resource for all sangha members.

item.title: January Book Club: 'The Heart of the Buddha's Teaching'
item.description:
I’d like to propose we read “The Heart of the Buddha’s Teaching” by Thich Nhat Hanh for our January/February book club cycle.

Why this book?

  • It’s accessible to newcomers but has depth for experienced practitioners
  • Thich Nhat Hanh’s writing style is clear and practical
  • It covers the core teachings in a way that connects to everyday life

Suggested schedule

  • Week 1-2: Part One — The Four Noble Truths (chapters 1-7)
  • Week 3-4: Part Two — The Noble Eightfold Path (chapters 8-16)
  • Week 5-6: Part Three — Other Buddhist Teachings (chapters 17-28)

We could discuss each section during our Wednesday evening sits. What does everyone think of this timeline?

item.title: About our Sangha
item.description:
About our Sangha

The Eau Claire Buddhist Sangha meets weekly on Tuesday evenings. We meditate together, listen to a recorded dharma talk from a teacher, brought by one of the members, and have a discussion about the teachings and meditation practice.

Each 90 minute gathering is divided into 30 minute segments.

  • 7:00 PM: Group meditation with instruction for newcomers
  • 7:30 PM: Recorded Dharma talk from a teacher presented by one of the members
  • 8:00 PM: Open discussion about the teachings and meditation practice

Cushions, chairs, and blankets are available for meditation support. Through practice, learning, and dialogue, we support our own and each others’ practice and intentions to follow the path to live a skillful, compassionate life.

item.title: Support the Sangha
item.description:
Dana: The Practice of Generosity

Dana is the Pali word for generosity — one of the foundational practices in Buddhism. Our sangha is sustained entirely through dana.

There is no fee to attend the weekly gatherings. All are invited to contribute what they can — whether through time, talent, or financial support — toward the flourishing of this community.

What Your Donations Support

  • Room rental at Unity of Eau Claire
  • Meditation cushions, chairs, and blankets
  • Library materials and dharma resources
  • Dharma talk recordings and media

Ways to Give

Online

PayPal Donations
